title: Wireless Design, Configuration, and Validation · Incident Repair Bay
summary: Design staff, guest, and point-to-point wireless service across crowded, regulated, and mobile spaces. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a secure wireless survey and deployment plan and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare relevant 802.11 capabilities across 2.4, 5, and 6 GHz bands, channel availability, range, and client support.
Choose channel, width, reuse, and band steering from regulatory domain, overlap, contention, capacity, and coverage.
Distinguish SSID, BSSID, ESS, hidden names, and client discovery without treating an SSID as a security control.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
Use a changed debug case derived from this module: network wireless. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Inspect the Net Wifi Standards Bands trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for compare relevant 802.11 capabilities across 2.4, 5, and 6 ghz bands, channel availability, range, and client support.

content.text: For Net Wifi Standards Bands, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Net Wifi Standards Bands precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.
Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed debug case derived from this module: network wireless. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A newer Wi-Fi generation guarantees every client uses its maximum speed.

lines.label: evidence

Artifact under review: a complete first version of a secure wireless survey and deployment plan while every earlier competency remains active.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Net Wifi Standards Bands, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a safe investigation headed "# Evidence: net-wireless-survey-roaming-6rcc4y" with a concrete hypothesis, one read-only network command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Net Wireless Survey Roaming into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: net-wireless-survey-roaming-6rcc4y
# hypothesis: [replace with one falsifiable probable cause]
# command: choose one safe read-only simulator command
# observation: [replace with the result that supports or rejects the hypothesis]

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Net Wireless Survey Roaming applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Perform predictive and measured surveys and verify RSSI, SNR, interference, utilization, roaming, and application outcomes.
